God’s instructions are a comfort to believers in this difficult world; they experience the presence of the living God through them: this makes them regret their absence from them. They are like a nest to the bird for their souls. Yet they are only a guarantee of the happiness of heaven; but how can people desire to enter that holy dwelling, who complain of God’s instructions as tiresome? Those are truly happy, who go forward and continue in practicing religion, with the strength of Jesus Christ's grace, from whom all our sufficiency comes. The travelers to the heavenly city may have to pass through many valleys of weeping and many thirsty deserts; but wells of salvation will be opened for them, and consolations sent for their support. Those who press forward in their Christian journey will find God adding grace to their graces. And those who grow in grace will be perfect in glory.

In all our prayers to God, we must desire that he would look on Christ, his Anointed One, and accept us for his sake: we must look to him with faith, and then God will favorably look upon the face of the Anointed. We dare not show our faces without him. The psalmist pleads love for God’s instructions. Let us consider one day in God's courts better than a thousand spent elsewhere; and deem the lowest place in his service preferable to the highest earthly honor. We are here in darkness, but if God is our God, he will be to us a Sun, to enlighten and enliven us, to guide and direct us. We are here in danger, but he will be to us a Shield, to secure us from the fiery darts that fly thick around us. Though he has not promised to give riches and dignities, he has promised to give grace and glory to all who seek them in his appointed way. And what is grace, but heaven begun below, in the knowledge, love, and service of God? What is glory, but the completion of this happiness, in being made like him, and in fully enjoying him forever? Let it be our care to walk uprightly, and then let us trust God to give us everything that is good for us. If we cannot go to the house of the Lord, we may go by faith to the Lord of the house; in him we will be happy, and may be at ease. That man is truly happy, whatever his outward circumstances may be, who trusts in the Lord of hosts, the God of Jacob.
